name: Umm al-Hasan
title: Legacy of Amir al-Mu'minin
shortDescription: Another daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was present in Karbala and endured the tragedies of Ashura and captivity with patience.
fullStory:
Umm al-Hasan bint Ali was one of the daughters of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and among the patient and pious ladies of the Bani Hashem family. Like her other sisters Zaynab, Umm Kulthum, Fatima, Khadija, and Ruqayya, she accompanied her brother Imam Hussain (AS) on the journey to Karbala.

On the day of Ashura, she witnessed the unjust martyrdom of her brothers and loved ones and, with a broken heart, took refuge in the tent along with the other women and children. When the enemy army attacked to loot the tents, she was also subjected to oppression and cruelty.

Throughout the route of captivity from Kufa to Sham, despite enduring numerous humiliations and hardships, Umm al-Hasan maintained her faith and perseverance and became known as a paragon of patience in history.

After returning to Medina, she spent the rest of her life in grief and sorrow, keeping the message of Ashura alive with her tears.
quote: (Addressing the enemies:) Woe unto you! You killed the Prophet's grandson and threw your families into Hellfire.
